shape_analysis.sigma_0_eq_11_shape: n = p^10 for a single prime p
shape_analysis.argument: σ₀ is multiplicative; (a₁+1)…(aₖ+1) = 11 with 11 prime forces k=1, a₁=10.
shape_analysis.impossibility_proof: σ(p^10) = 1+p+...+p^10 ≡ 1 (mod p), so p ∤ σ(p^10), so p^10 ∤ σ(p^10), so n = p^10 is never multiperfect.
shape_analysis.sigma_0_eq_13: Same — n = p^12, trivially never multiperfect

verdict: σ₀(n)=11 odd multiperfect: TRIVIALLY IMPOSSIBLE via 1-line p-adic argument. The gap (no odd multiperfect with σ₀=11) is provably TRUE but NOT a substantial result — the impossibility is elementary. The user's framing (3 shapes: p^10, p^4·q^2, p^2·q·r) was incorrect: 11 prime forces SHAPE = p^10 only. The 3-shape case-split corresponds to σ₀=12 (4·3) or σ₀=10 (5·2) or σ₀=15 (5·3), not σ₀=11.
actionable_sketch_targets: (A) σ₀(n)=11 impossibility — TRIVIAL (1-line proof); not a closure target.
(B) Reframe as σ₀=12 OR σ₀=15 — these have 3-shape splits and are nontrivial.
(C) Σ shape (p^4·q^2 or p^2·q·r) — these are FROM σ₀=15, not σ₀=11.
recommended_gap: Submit IMPOSSIBILITY: ¬ ∃ n, n odd ∧ σ(n) = k·n ∧ σ₀(n) = 11. Aristotle's slot737 σ₀ multiplicativity DNA applies but the argument is even simpler — case-split (a+1)...(aₖ+1)=11 forces single-prime; (1+p+...+p^10) ≡ 1 (mod p). Bare conjecture sketch.
